---
title: "UNHCR WASH Capstone Project"
author: "Matlove23"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format: html
editor: visual
toc: true
toc-location: left
toc-depth: 2
number-sections: true
bibliography: references.bib
execute:
  warning: false
  echo: true
---
# Introduction

This report analyzes UNHCR WASH assessment data collected from refugee settings.  
The data captures key indicators such as water quantity, water quality, sanitation access, hygiene conditions, and population characteristics.  
The goal of this capstone is to prepare the data, analyze trends, and draw insights that inform humanitarian WASH programming.

# Methods

The dataset was obtained from the UNHCR WASH raw dataset stored in the `data/raw/` folder.  
Data was imported, cleaned, renamed, filtered, and transformed using `tidyverse` tools.  
Processed analysis-ready data is stored in `data/processed/` along with a README and a data dictionary.
---

title: "My Data Analysis Report" format: html ---

## Analysis Goals

In this project, my goal is to analyse the uploaded dataset to understand key patterns and insights. Specifically, I aim to identify trends, make comparisons, generate visualisations, and answer the main research questions related to the data.

## Description of the Data

## The data I have uploaded contains information about \[brief description\]. It includes variables such as \[list important variables\]. The dataset will be used for cleaning, exploration, visualisation, and statistical analysis throughout this report.

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(here)
# Load raw data
raw_data <- read_csv(here("data/raw/unhcrwash.csv"))
# Preview the data
glimpse(raw_data)
# Load processed data
processed_data <- read_csv(here::here("data/processed/my_processed_data.csv"))
names(processed_data)
length(names(processed_data))
dictionary <- tibble(
  variable_name = names(processed_data),
  description = rep("To be completed", length(names(processed_data)))
)

write_csv(dictionary, here::here("data/processed/data_dictionary.csv"))
#| label: summary-table
#| tbl-cap: "Summary statistics for key variables."
#| warning: false
# Load necessary packages
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
colnames(processed_data)

summary_table <- processed_data %>%
  summarise(
    mean_liters = mean(water_lppd, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_liters = median(water_lppd, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_liters = sd(water_lppd, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_toilets = mean(toilets, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

kable(summary_table, caption = "Key WASH indicators summary.")

#| label: fig-water
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of water access (liters per person per day)."
#| warning: false

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(processed_data, aes(x = water_lppd)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  labs(
    x = "Liters per person per day",
    y = "Count",
    title = "Distribution of Water Access in Refugee Camps"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_x_continuous()
#| label: fig-quality
#| fig-cap: "Water quality status across countries."
#| warning: false
#| label: fig-quality
#| fig-cap: "Water quality status across countries."
#| warning: false

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Make sure water quality is a factor
processed_data <- raw_data %>%
  select(form_id, location_name, start_date, liters_per_person_per_day, households_with_toilet) %>%
  rename(
    household_id = form_id,
    camp_name = location_name,
    water_lppd = liters_per_person_per_day,
    toilets = households_with_toilet
  )
processed_data <- raw_data %>%
  select(
    form_id,
    location_name,
    start_date,
    liters_per_person_per_day,
    households_with_toilet,
    chlorinated_safe_water_quality,
    country
  ) %>%
  rename(
    household_id = form_id,
    camp_name = location_name,
    water_lppd = liters_per_person_per_day,
    toilets = households_with_toilet,
    water_quality = chlorinated_safe_water_quality
  )
processed_data <- processed_data %>%
  mutate(water_quality = factor(water_quality))

# Plot
ggplot(processed_data, aes(x = country, fill = water_quality)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    x = "Country",
    y = "Number of Records",
    fill = "Water Quality"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))









```

\`\`\`{r} #\| warning: true raw_data \<- read_csv(here::here("data/raw/unhcrwash.csv")) \# Load libraries library(tidyverse) library(lubridate) library(here)

# Load dataset

raw_data \<- read_csv(here("data/raw/unhcrwash.csv")) clean_data \<- raw_data %\>% rename( form_id = form_id, start_date = start_date, end_date = end_date, location_id = location_id, location_name = location_name, country = country, post_emergency = post_emergency,

```         
persons_per_handpump          = persons_per_handpump,
persons_per_tap               = persons_per_tap,

liters_ppd                    = liters_per_person_per_day,
non_chlorinated_0_cfu         = non_chlorinated_0_cfu,
chlorinated_water_quality     = chlorinated_safe_water_quality,

households_with_toilet        = households_with_toilet,
persons_per_toilet            = persons_per_toilet,
persons_per_shower            = persons_per_shower,
persons_per_hygiene_promoter  = persons_per_hygiene_promoter,

refugee_population            = refugee_pop,
reporting_monthly             = reporting_monthly,

liters_pp_household           = liters_per_person_household,
potable_storage_10l           = potable_water_storage_10l,

protected_water_sources       = protected_water_sources,
menstrual_hygiene_satisfaction = menstrual_hygiene_satisfaction,

household_toilet              = household_toilet,
defecate_in_toilet            = defecate_in_toilet,
access_to_soap                = access_to_soap,
solid_waste_disposal_access   = solid_waste_disposal_access,

reporting_annual              = reporting_annual
```

) #----------------------------------------- clean_data \<- clean_data %\>% select( form_id, location_id, location_name, country, start_date, end_date, post_emergency, persons_per_handpump, persons_per_tap, liters_ppd, non_chlorinated_0_cfu, chlorinated_water_quality, households_with_toilet, persons_per_toilet, persons_per_shower, persons_per_hygiene_promoter, refugee_population, reporting_monthly, liters_pp_household, potable_storage_10l, protected_water_sources, menstrual_hygiene_satisfaction, household_toilet, defecate_in_toilet, access_to_soap, solid_waste_disposal_access, reporting_annual )

#----------------------------------------- \# 3. HANDLE MISSING VALUES #----------------------------------------- \# Remove rows with critical missing values clean_data \<- clean_data %\>% drop_na(form_id, location_name, liters_ppd, households_with_toilet)

#----------------------------------------- \# 4. JOIN WITH OTHER DATASETS (EXAMPLE) #----------------------------------------- \# Example: join with district population data \# population_data \<- read_csv(here("data/raw/district_population.csv")) \# clean_data \<- clean_data %\>% \# left_join(population_data, by = "location_name")

#----------------------------------------- \# 5. CREATE NEW VARIABLES #----------------------------------------- clean_data \<- clean_data %\>% mutate( start_date = ymd(start_date), end_date = ymd(end_date), year = year(start_date), month = month(start_date, label = TRUE),

```         
# Water classification
water_category = case_when(
  protected_water_sources %in% c("Borehole", "Piped Water", "Protected Well") ~ "Improved",
  TRUE ~ "Unimproved"
),

# Sanitation classification
sanitation_category = case_when(
  households_with_toilet > 0 ~ "Improved",
  TRUE ~ "Unimproved"
),

# Example: water availability per person per day
water_per_person_day = liters_ppd,

# Example: total household water storage
total_household_storage_liters = liters_pp_household * households_with_toilet
```

)

#----------------------------------------- \# 6. FINAL CLEANED DATA PREVIEW #----------------------------------------- glimpse(clean_data) library(tidyverse) library(here)

# Process the data

processed_data \<- raw_data %\>% \# Select only relevant columns (replace with actual column names) select(form_id, location_name, start_date, liters_per_person_per_day, households_with_toilet) %\>%

\# Remove rows with missing values in critical columns filter(!is.na(form_id), !is.na(location_name)) %\>%

\# Rename columns if needed rename( household_id = form_id, camp_name = location_name, water_lppd = liters_per_person_per_day, toilets = households_with_toilet )

# Save the processed dataset

write_csv( processed_data, here::here("data/processed/my_processed_data.csv") ) \# Summarize water availability by camp water_summary \<- clean_data %\>% group_by(location_name) %\>% summarise(avg_liters_ppd = mean(liters_ppd, na.rm = TRUE)) #\| fig.cap="Average liters of water per person per day by camp", fig.align='center' \# Plot water availability ggplot(water_summary, aes(x = reorder(location_name, avg_liters_ppd), y = avg_liters_ppd, fill = avg_liters_ppd)) + geom_col() + coord_flip() + scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue") + labs(x = "Camp Name", y = "Average Liters per Person per Day", fill = "Liters") + theme_minimal()

```         

\`\`\`{r} #\| warning: true raw_data \<- read_csv(here::here("data/raw/unhcrwash.csv")) \# Load libraries library(tidyverse) library(lubridate) library(here)

# Load dataset

raw_data \<- read_csv(here("data/raw/unhcrwash.csv")) clean_data \<- raw_data %\>% rename( form_id = form_id, start_date = start_date, end_date = end_date, location_id = location_id, location_name = location_name, country = country, post_emergency = post_emergency,
```

persons_per_handpump = persons_per_handpump, persons_per_tap = persons_per_tap,

liters_ppd = liters_per_person_per_day, non_chlorinated_0_cfu = non_chlorinated_0_cfu, chlorinated_water_quality = chlorinated_safe_water_quality,

households_with_toilet = households_with_toilet, persons_per_toilet = persons_per_toilet, persons_per_shower = persons_per_shower, persons_per_hygiene_promoter = persons_per_hygiene_promoter,

refugee_population = refugee_pop, reporting_monthly = reporting_monthly,

liters_pp_household = liters_per_person_household, potable_storage_10l = potable_water_storage_10l,

protected_water_sources = protected_water_sources, menstrual_hygiene_satisfaction = menstrual_hygiene_satisfaction,

household_toilet = household_toilet, defecate_in_toilet = defecate_in_toilet, access_to_soap = access_to_soap, solid_waste_disposal_access = solid_waste_disposal_access,

reporting_annual = reporting_annual

```         

) #----------------------------------------- clean_data \<- clean_data %\>% select( form_id, location_id, location_name, country, start_date, end_date, post_emergency, persons_per_handpump, persons_per_tap, liters_ppd, non_chlorinated_0_cfu, chlorinated_water_quality, households_with_toilet, persons_per_toilet, persons_per_shower, persons_per_hygiene_promoter, refugee_population, reporting_monthly, liters_pp_household, potable_storage_10l, protected_water_sources, menstrual_hygiene_satisfaction, household_toilet, defecate_in_toilet, access_to_soap, solid_waste_disposal_access, reporting_annual )

#----------------------------------------- \# 3. HANDLE MISSING VALUES #----------------------------------------- \# Remove rows with critical missing values clean_data \<- clean_data %\>% drop_na(form_id, location_name, liters_ppd, households_with_toilet)

#----------------------------------------- \# 4. JOIN WITH OTHER DATASETS (EXAMPLE) #----------------------------------------- \# Example: join with district population data \# population_data \<- read_csv(here("data/raw/district_population.csv")) \# clean_data \<- clean_data %\>% \# left_join(population_data, by = "location_name")

#----------------------------------------- \# 5. CREATE NEW VARIABLES #----------------------------------------- clean_data \<- clean_data %\>% mutate( start_date = ymd(start_date), end_date = ymd(end_date), year = year(start_date), month = month(start_date, label = TRUE),
```

# Water classification

water_category = case_when( protected_water_sources %in% c("Borehole", "Piped Water", "Protected Well") \~ "Improved", TRUE \~ "Unimproved" ),

# Sanitation classification

sanitation_category = case_when( households_with_toilet \> 0 \~ "Improved", TRUE \~ "Unimproved" ),

# Example: water availability per person per day

water_per_person_day = liters_ppd,

# Example: total household water storage

total_household_storage_liters = liters_pp_household \* households_with_toilet

```         

)

#----------------------------------------- \# 6. FINAL CLEANED DATA PREVIEW #----------------------------------------- glimpse(clean_data) library(tidyverse) library(here)

# Process the data

processed_data \<- raw_data %\>% \# Select only relevant columns (replace with actual column names) select(form_id, location_name, start_date, liters_per_person_per_day, households_with_toilet) %\>%

\# Remove rows with missing values in critical columns filter(!is.na(form_id), !is.na(location_name)) %\>%

\# Rename columns if needed rename( household_id = form_id, camp_name = location_name, water_lppd = liters_per_person_per_day, toilets = households_with_toilet )

# Save the processed dataset

write_csv( processed_data, here::here("data/processed/my_processed_data.csv") ) \# Summarize water availability by camp water_summary \<- clean_data %\>% group_by(location_name) %\>% summarise(avg_liters_ppd = mean(liters_ppd, na.rm = TRUE)) #\| fig.cap="Average liters of water per person per day by camp", fig.align='center' \# Plot water availability ggplot(water_summary, aes(x = reorder(location_name, avg_liters_ppd), y = avg_liters_ppd, fill = avg_liters_ppd)) + geom_col() + coord_flip() + scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue") + labs(x = "Camp Name", y = "Average Liters per Person per Day", fill = "Liters") + theme_minimal()

# Load data
raw_data <- read_csv(here::here("data/raw/unhcrwash.csv"))
```

\`\`\`{r} #\| warning: true raw_data \<- read_csv(here::here("data/raw/unhcrwash.csv")) \# Load libraries library(tidyverse) library(lubridate) library(here)

# Load dataset

raw_data \<- read_csv(here("data/raw/unhcrwash.csv")) clean_data \<- raw_data %\>% rename( form_id = form_id, start_date = start_date, end_date = end_date, location_id = location_id, location_name = location_name, country = country, post_emergency = post_emergency,

```         
persons_per_handpump          = persons_per_handpump,
persons_per_tap               = persons_per_tap,

liters_ppd                    = liters_per_person_per_day,
non_chlorinated_0_cfu         = non_chlorinated_0_cfu,
chlorinated_water_quality     = chlorinated_safe_water_quality,

households_with_toilet        = households_with_toilet,
persons_per_toilet            = persons_per_toilet,
persons_per_shower            = persons_per_shower,
persons_per_hygiene_promoter  = persons_per_hygiene_promoter,

refugee_population            = refugee_pop,
reporting_monthly             = reporting_monthly,

liters_pp_household           = liters_per_person_household,
potable_storage_10l           = potable_water_storage_10l,

protected_water_sources       = protected_water_sources,
menstrual_hygiene_satisfaction = menstrual_hygiene_satisfaction,

household_toilet              = household_toilet,
defecate_in_toilet            = defecate_in_toilet,
access_to_soap                = access_to_soap,
solid_waste_disposal_access   = solid_waste_disposal_access,

reporting_annual              = reporting_annual
```

) #----------------------------------------- clean_data \<- clean_data %\>% select( form_id, location_id, location_name, country, start_date, end_date, post_emergency, persons_per_handpump, persons_per_tap, liters_ppd, non_chlorinated_0_cfu, chlorinated_water_quality, households_with_toilet, persons_per_toilet, persons_per_shower, persons_per_hygiene_promoter, refugee_population, reporting_monthly, liters_pp_household, potable_storage_10l, protected_water_sources, menstrual_hygiene_satisfaction, household_toilet, defecate_in_toilet, access_to_soap, solid_waste_disposal_access, reporting_annual )

#----------------------------------------- \# 3. HANDLE MISSING VALUES #----------------------------------------- \# Remove rows with critical missing values clean_data \<- clean_data %\>% drop_na(form_id, location_name, liters_ppd, households_with_toilet)

#----------------------------------------- \# 4. JOIN WITH OTHER DATASETS (EXAMPLE) #----------------------------------------- \# Example: join with district population data \# population_data \<- read_csv(here("data/raw/district_population.csv")) \# clean_data \<- clean_data %\>% \# left_join(population_data, by = "location_name")

#----------------------------------------- \# 5. CREATE NEW VARIABLES #----------------------------------------- clean_data \<- clean_data %\>% mutate( start_date = ymd(start_date), end_date = ymd(end_date), year = year(start_date), month = month(start_date, label = TRUE),

```         
# Water classification
water_category = case_when(
  protected_water_sources %in% c("Borehole", "Piped Water", "Protected Well") ~ "Improved",
  TRUE ~ "Unimproved"
),

# Sanitation classification
sanitation_category = case_when(
  households_with_toilet > 0 ~ "Improved",
  TRUE ~ "Unimproved"
),

# Example: water availability per person per day
water_per_person_day = liters_ppd,

# Example: total household water storage
total_household_storage_liters = liters_pp_household * households_with_toilet
```

)

#----------------------------------------- \# 6. FINAL CLEANED DATA PREVIEW #----------------------------------------- glimpse(clean_data) library(tidyverse) library(here)

# Process the data

processed_data \<- raw_data %\>% \# Select only relevant columns (replace with actual column names) select(form_id, location_name, start_date, liters_per_person_per_day, households_with_toilet) %\>%

\# Remove rows with missing values in critical columns filter(!is.na(form_id), !is.na(location_name)) %\>%

\# Rename columns if needed rename( household_id = form_id, camp_name = location_name, water_lppd = liters_per_person_per_day, toilets = households_with_toilet )

# Save the processed dataset

write_csv( processed_data, here::here("data/processed/my_processed_data.csv") ) \# Summarize water availability by camp water_summary \<- clean_data %\>% group_by(location_name) %\>% summarise(avg_liters_ppd = mean(liters_ppd, na.rm = TRUE)) #\| fig.cap="Average liters of water per person per day by camp", fig.align='center' \# Plot water availability ggplot(water_summary, aes(x = reorder(location_name, avg_liters_ppd), y = avg_liters_ppd, fill = avg_liters_ppd)) + geom_col() + coord_flip() + scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue") + labs(x = "Camp Name", y = "Average Liters per Person per Day", fill = "Liters") + theme_minimal()
